quote: "Believe you can, and you're halfway there." – Theodore Roosevelt
"Believe you can, and you're halfway there." – Theodore Roosevelt
To cultivate true connections, first refine yourself; for the essence of every bond lies not in desire, but in the character you offer the world.
In the embrace of time's fleeting hand, let your heart weigh the measure of each moment; for death reveals the worth of a life lived without regret.
In the court of existence, seek not the approval of others; only the unyielding strength of your own virtue shall endure when all else fades.
Adversity does not create character; it merely unveils what was already hidden, a mirror reflecting our true steadiness in the storm of existence.
In the stillness of existence, remember: what you cherish lies within; the tempest outside is mere illusion, immutable to your essence.
